calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Sister State Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-22-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. SHARON ROSSI, Esq. of Rogers Joseph O'Donnell for Plaint
Atty. JAIME RITTON, Esq. of Mound Cotton Wollan & Greengrass LLP (tel: 510-616-9371) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT DONE GLOBAL, INC.'s Motion To Vacate Sister State Judgment.

Matter argued. Plaintiff's counsel cites new authority not previously briefed. The Court passes the matter to review the cited case. The Court recalls the matter. Matter further argued. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling as modified:

Defendant Done Global. Inc.'s motion to vacate sister state judgment is granted. Done Global has established its entitlement to vacation of this court's judgment on the District of Columbia Superior Court's judgment per Code of Civil Procedure 1710.40. This motion is not untimely. While the motion was not filed until more than 30 days after the notice of appearance of counsel for Done Global and that notice did not specify that the appearance was only a limited one to contest jurisdiction of this court, under the circumstances of this case the notice of appearance is properly construed as a special appearance limited to contesting jurisdiction and thus did not trigger the 30-day time frame to file a motion to vacate the sister state judgment. Done Global has also shown that service on Ms. He of the sister state judgment was not proper service on Done Global because, at the time of the service on Ms. He, she was not an officer or general manager of Done Global.
Code of Civil Procedure section 1710.40 does not preclude the defense of lack of service of the sister state judgment on a defendant. This Court is making this ruling to avoid constitutional issues regarding due process. In the event that the defense of lack of service of the sister state judgment on a defendant is precluded by Code of Civil Procedure section 1710.40, then this Court finds that Code of Civil Procedure section 1710.40 is unconstitutional because it seeks to avoid due process.

Order submitted.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/HEK)
